qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: Consider the recursive algorithm given below: procedure bubblesort (n); var i,j: index; temp : item; begin for i:=1 to n-1 do if A[i] > A[i+1] then begin temp := A[i]; A[i] := A[i+1]; A[i+1] := temp; end;

qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: The recurrence relation that arises in relation with the complexity of binary search is: A. B. C. D.

qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: The recurrence relation has the solution equal to A. B. C. D. None of the above

qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: Consider the following function. Function F(n, m:integer):integer; begin if (n<=0) or (m<=0) then F:=1 else F:F(n-1, m) + F(n-1, m-1); end; Use the recurrence relation to answer the following questions. Assume that are positive integers. Write only the answers without any explanation.
